feed.title: European Journal of Applied Linguistics
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from European Journal of Applied Linguistics (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Editorial
entry.summary: Authors: Grégory Miras, Kristin Bührig, Jan D. ten Thije DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2026-2001 Pages: Page range: 1-1

entry.title: The making of European Applied Linguistics: Tracing global origins, examining local identities
entry.summary: Authors: Grégory Miras DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0051 Pages: Page range: 2-32

entry.title: Demarcating the boundaries of non-belonging: Gender, integration, and the Burkini in German newspaper comments
entry.summary: Authors: Emily E. Davis DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0018 Pages: Page range: 33-61

entry.title: The use of motivational strategies: Perspectives of Tunisian EFL teachers and students
entry.summary: Authors: Bochra Kouraichi DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0005 Pages: Page range: 62-77

entry.title: Translating (in) the Public Service – When interpreting facilitates migrants’ understanding of the institutional context
entry.summary: Authors: Daniele Urlotti DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0011 Pages: Page range: 78-100

entry.title: Blended learning for autonomy development in heterogeneous FLE classes: A lever for developing learners’ transversal competencies?
entry.summary: Authors: Paul Pouzergues DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0003 Pages: Page range: 101-131

entry.title: Development of a cognitive-linguistic assessment battery for L1 Italian learners of L2 Chinese with and without literacy impairments
entry.summary: Authors: Nicoletta Cirota DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0032 Pages: Page range: 132-159

entry.title: To talk or not to talk? Willingness to communicate in English as an L2: Investigating Greek learners’ perspectives and their teachers’ perspectives and strategies in Greece
entry.summary: Authors: Artenisa Topouzi DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0035 Pages: Page range: 160-181

entry.title: Knowledge of receptive English morphology among Finnish university students: L2 learners’ morpheme recognition and its implications to vocabulary learning
entry.summary: Authors: Anna Reini DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2024-0058 Pages: Page range: 182-209

entry.title: Learner-initiated grammar explanations in L2 French classroom interaction
entry.summary: Authors: Loanne Janin DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2024-0060 Pages: Page range: 210-230

entry.title: Learner agency and symbiotic agency with technology in language learning with digital applications
entry.summary: Authors: Elisa Räsänen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2025-0002 Pages: Page range: 231-259

entry.title: Social ‘integration’ and language learning at two European universities: Students’ perceptions of institutional and student-led opportunities
entry.summary: Authors: Andreana Pastena DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/eujal-2024-0050 Pages: Page range: 260-286
